# Accessibility, Animation, and Cross-Device Quality — Modern Landing Page Redesign

## User Story

**As a** visitor using any device, browser, or assistive technology,  
**I want to** experience the landing page fully and without barriers regardless of my context or preferences,  
**so that** I can access all information and interact with all features comfortably.

---

## Description

This story covers the cross-cutting quality concerns for the landing page redesign: the orchestrated page-load stagger reveal animation, `prefers-reduced-motion` compliance, WCAG AA contrast across all sections, and consistent responsive behaviour across mobile, tablet, and desktop breakpoints. These requirements span all page sections and should be validated as an integrated whole once all section stories are complete.

**Business value:** Accessibility compliance reduces legal risk and expands the addressable audience. Responsive quality builds credibility with technical evaluators who may review the site on multiple devices.

---

## Scenario

### Scenario 1: Full page-load stagger animation

**Given** a visitor loads the landing page for the first time  
**And** their browser does not have `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` set  
**When** the page finishes loading  
**Then** page sections animate in with a single orchestrated stagger reveal sequence  
**And** the animation begins with the hero and progresses down the page  
**And** no section content is visible before its reveal animation completes (no layout flicker)

### Scenario 2: Reduced-motion preference across all sections

**Given** a visitor has `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` set  
**When** the landing page loads  
**Then** all stagger reveals, hover transitions, and chip animations across every section are fully suppressed  
**And** all content is immediately visible and accessible on load  
**And** no sections are hidden or delayed due to animation logic

### Scenario 3: Keyboard-only navigation through the full page

**Given** a visitor navigates using only the keyboard (Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, Space)  
**When** they traverse the landing page  
**Then** every interactive element (CTAs, cards, badges, footer links, email CTA) is reachable via Tab  
**And** each focused element displays a visible focus ring  
**And** no focus traps exist outside of modal-level interactions  
**And** skip-navigation or landmark regions allow bypassing repeated blocks

### Scenario 4: Screen reader traversal

**Given** a visitor uses a screen reader  
**When** they navigate the landing page  
**Then** decorative elements (icons, dividers, background textures) are hidden from the accessibility tree  
**And** section headings form a logical outline (`h1` → `h2` → `h3`)  
**And** the reading order matches the visual order

### Scenario 5: Colour contrast check across accent elements

**Given** any section containing text over the accent gradient or tinted backgrounds  
**When** the page is rendered  
**Then** all text meets WCAG AA contrast ratio (4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large/bold text)  
**And** this applies to: hero chip labels, CTA button labels, Contact CTA panel text, and process step numbers

### Scenario 6: Responsive integrity across all sections

**Given** a visitor resizes the browser or visits on a mobile device  
**When** the viewport transitions through mobile, tablet, and desktop breakpoints  
**Then** no section produces horizontal overflow or a horizontal scrollbar  
**And** all text remains readable without zooming  
**And** all interactive elements remain tap-friendly (minimum 44×44px touch target on mobile)

---

## Acceptance Criteria

### Orchestrated Animation
- [ ] A single page-load stagger animation system is used across all sections (not per-section ad hoc CSS)
- [ ] Animation sequence order matches the visual top-to-bottom reading order of the page
- [ ] No content is invisible or clipped at rest; animation uses opacity/transform only (no `display: none` toggling)
- [ ] Animation timing values are sourced from the shared motion timing CSS tokens

### Reduced-Motion Compliance
- [ ] The `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` media query is handled globally (not duplicated per section)
- [ ] When set: all animations, transitions, and hover effects site-wide are suppressed
- [ ] When set: all page content is fully visible immediately without any delayed reveal

### Keyboard Accessibility
- [ ] All interactive elements are reachable via Tab navigation
- [ ] Focus indicators are visible on all interactive elements (no `outline: none` without replacement)
- [ ] Tab order follows the logical reading sequence of the page
- [ ] Anchor link buttons (CTAs, footer links) are activated by Enter/Space

### Screen Reader Compatibility
- [ ] Decorative SVG icons use `aria-hidden="true"`
- [ ] CSS background textures and pseudo-elements carry no accessible name
- [ ] Heading hierarchy: single `<h1>` for hero; `<h2>` for section headings; `<h3>` or lower for card/step titles
- [ ] All `<img>` elements (if any) have descriptive alt text

### Colour Contrast
- [ ] Hero headline and sub-copy: ≥4.5:1 contrast on hero background
- [ ] CTA button labels: ≥4.5:1 on button background
- [ ] Contact CTA panel text: ≥4.5:1 on accent gradient background
- [ ] Service card text (default and hover state): ≥4.5:1
- [ ] Process step numbers on accent gradient: ≥3:1 (large text)

### Responsive Quality
- [ ] No horizontal overflow or scrollbar at 320px, 375px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px viewport widths
- [ ] All touch targets on mobile are at least 44×44px (CTAs, navigation links, badges)
- [ ] Text does not overflow card or chip boundaries at any breakpoint
- [ ] Images or decorative elements do not cause layout shifts on load
